original: Player: Tavern
Tyrion: So...[p/] Is it just me,[p/] or does this hallway look similar to the one in your family's tavern?
Celeste: Well,[p/] most taverns use the same architecture.[p/] So, I don't think it's [i]that[/i] strange.
Tyrion: No,[p/] but it looks [i]exactly[/i] like the McCoy Tavern.[p/] It's uncanny.
Tyrion: It's as if whoever made this tavern decided to just reuse the same design.
Celeste: Hey,[p/] making unique designs is difficult, Tyrion.[p/] [i]And[/i] it costs money.
Celeste: Do you really think developers should be spending resources on a location that you'll only see once?
Tyrion: I don't know...[p/] It just seems kind of lazy.
Celeste: Well, if it bothers you so much,[p/][s/] why don't [i]you[/i] pay for the assets required to make a new one?
I decided to drop the matter.
Player: End Door
Tyrion: Is this your room, Aster?
Aster: Yes,[p/] it's quite cozy.
Aster: I'm very pleased with how much personality the craftsmanship has.
Aster: It's nothing like any of the resorts I've stayed at.[p/] It's far more rustic.
Tyrion: Hm...
Player: Back
Player: Aster de Wyverngarde
Tyrion: So, Your Highness—
Aster: Please, there's no need for that.[p/] Just call me "Aster".
He couldn't be serious, could he?
After I got a good look at him,[p/] he looked sincere—[p/]a little [i]too[/i] sincere.
He carried himself with the poise of a royal,[p/] but there was also a warm kindness and humility in his eyes.
He sounded too good to be true,[p/] but maybe I was just too jaded by the other aristocrats I'd met.
Tyrion: What is the Crown Prince doing in a tavern like this, anyway?
Aster: Ah,[p/] the Kingsguard were being far too overbearing.
Aster: They were insistent that I return to the capital,[p/] but I'd been planning this trip to Aurinax for days.
Aster: I found an opportunity to sneak away from them and hired a guide to show me around the city.
Tyrion: Are you talking about Noah Morrison?
Aster: Ah, yes.[p/] Mr. Morrison.
Aster: He told me quite a lot about the industrial complexities of Aurinax[p/] and even more about the workers' unions.
Celeste: ...Is it really okay for you to disappear like that?
Celeste: The Kingsguard are probably freaking out over losing track of you.
Aster: Ah,[p/] they worry far too much.[p/] I can take care of myself.
Aster: I may not look it,[p/] but I'm one of the most powerful mages in this kingdom.
Tyrion: That sounds awfully cocky,[p/] don't you think?
Aster: Hm...[p/] You think so?
There was so much optimism behind those eyes.[p/] How sheltered was this guy?
Player: What did you see?
Tyrion: Anyways,[p/] Mr. Morrison told us you were present when the accident occurred.
Aster: Ah, it was quite dreadful what happened.
Tyrion: We only have one witness who actually saw Justin fall into that vat.
Tyrion: I was wondering if you saw anything she didn't.
Aster: I didn't see much, I'm afraid.[p/] It all happened very quickly.
Aster: If he weren't yelling at that animal,[p/] I might not have even noticed him.
Tyrion: ...There was an animal up on the catwalk, too?
Aster: It looked to be some kind of hairless rodent,[p/] but it was abnormally large.
Aster: I think he may have been trying to catch it.[p/] He even grabbed it at one point.
Tyrion: ...Was he facing towards Dracogen's office when he was yelling at this rodent?
Aster: I think so.[p/] Noah pointed out the office window earlier.[p/] It allowed Stefanic to oversee the refinery.
Tyrion: Did you see anyone else up on the catwalk?
Aster: No,[p/] my view of the catwalk was limited by my position.
Ugh...[p/] This whole time,[p/] I thought Justin had been yelling at the Phantom,[p/] but it turned out he was just yelling at a rat.
It seemed that the only person who spotted the thief that day was Justin.
Player: The Accident
Tyrion: Did you see the moment that Justin fell in?[p/] Was he pushed off the catwalk by a spell?
Aster: I'm not quite sure,[p/] but I definitely saw something magical.
Aster: There was a flash of magical light centered on his position.
Aster: The magical energy took the shape of a prism before blowing apart the guardrail.
Aster: Justin lost his balance afterward and fell into the vat.
Tyrion: Did you see what the spell looked like?[p/] Do you think you could identify it for us?
Aster: The problem is that I'm almost sure that it [i]wasn't[/i] a spell.
Aster: It was the effect of some sort of artificery.
Tyrion: ...How can you be so sure?
Aster: It's difficult for someone who's not a mage to understand.
Aster: When a mage casts a spell,[p/] they draw and harness the aether from within their own body.
Aster: Artificery, on the other hand,[p/] draws aether from the natural ley lines that run through the ground.
Aster: I am someone who has practiced arcane magic for my entire life.
Aster: I've slowly learned how to sense how aether moves around me.
Celeste: You can do that, too?[p/] I thought I was the only one.
Aster: Oh,[p/] that talent is quite a rarity—[p/]even among the royal family.
Aster: I suppose I shouldn't expect anything less from the Crimson Demon.
Celeste: Stop calling me that.
Player: What spell was cast on Justin?
Tyrion: So you could feel the aether was moving towards the source of that spell.
Aster: Yes,[p/] it was quite distinct because of how high up he was.
Aster: I could sense the aether rising out of the ground to his position on the catwalk.
Celeste: So the guardrail was broken apart by this artificery.
Celeste: Is that why there weren't any traces of magic up there?
Tyrion: No...[p/] Artificery simply replicates the effects of an arcane spell.
Tyrion: They leave traces of magic,[p/] just like spells cast by a mage do.
Tyrion: That also means our culprit wasn't necessarily a mage.[p/] [i]Anyone[/i] could have used a device like that.
Aster: ...Not necessarily.
Tyrion: What do you mean?
Aster: The amount of aether being drawn was considerable.[p/] It was of a much a higher quantity than what most artificery devices are capable of.
Aster: Whatever that device was,[p/] it was quite advanced and dangerous.[p/] Something like that wouldn't be commonly available to the public.
The military would definitely have access to a weapon like that, too...
It pointed to Lucio Steelwind,[p/] but that just made his actions during the trial all the more bizarre.
Player: Lucio Steelwind
Tyrion: ...What can you tell me about Grand General Steelwind?
Aster: The Grand General is a difficult man to speak of.
Aster: In recent days,[p/] his name alone carries the weight of thousands of slaughtered innocents.
Tyrion: I take it you don't care much for him?
Aster: Not many do.[p/] Even the most heartless monster would see little sense in his bloody conquest in the countryside.
Aster: I was surprised when he took the stand at the trial yesterday,[p/] but I'm sure it's just another plot to undermine the other pillar families.
Aster: I had really hoped my arranged marriage to Lady Steelwind would sate his thirst for power,[p/] but it seems to have just made it worse.
Celeste: Wait...[p/] What did you just say?
Aster: Ah,[p/] of course.[p/] I suppose most outside of the nobility wouldn't be aware of it.
Aster: My father and the Grand General have arranged for me to marry Lady Steelwind.
Celeste: What?![p/][s/] She never told me she was engaged to the [i]Crown Prince[/i].
Aster: I wouldn't blame her.[p/] She's not exactly pleased with our arrangement.
Aster: That woman despises me.
Tyrion: ...What?
Aster: Our marriage is purely political—[p/]a way to unify the royal family with the upper brass of the military.
Aster: I admire her desire to bring prosperity to the commonfolk,[p/] but I unfortunately seem to represent everything she hates about this kingdom.
Aster: She can't even stand speaking to me.[p/] Every time I'm around her,[p/] she can barely contain her contempt.
Celeste: Oh no...
Aster: But I've made my peace with that.[p/] She's always cared more about her duty than anything else.
Aster: When I take the throne,[p/] I will build her a compassionate kingdom that she can take pride in.
...I really wanted to believe him,[p/] but I knew better than to trust empty promises like that.
I guess only time would tell what kind of ruler he would be.
Player: The Stolen Item
Player: BACK
Tyrion: Aster, I've been hearing an awful lot about how the serial robber stole that item from your ship.
Aster: Ah, that.[p/] The Kingsguard have been scrambling around trying to find it.
Tyrion: Would you be able to tell me what they stole?
Aster: Hm...[p/] I don't know.
Tyrion: I've been having a lot of trouble pinning down what the thief was after.
Tyrion: If they infiltrated your ship,[p/] they were taking a gigantic risk.[p/] They wouldn't have done that without a good reason.
Aster: ...
Aster: They stole several high-capacity arcane crystals attuned to the school of [b]evocation[/b].
Tyrion: Arcane crystals are pretty common, aren't they?[p/][s/] They're what draw and provide power to artificery devices.
Aster: The problem is that they're attuned to [b]evocation[/b]:[p/] the most raw and destructive school of magic.
Aster: Artificery that uses [b]evocation[/b] is heavily supervised by the kingdom,[p/] and most devices that use them are extremely weak.
Aster: Those crystals were a shipment addressed to Von Sanctus Industries.[p/] They were meant to be received at port and used to construct weapons for use by the military.
Tyrion: Are these crystals really that valuable?
Aster: They have a large capacity to channel [b]evocation[/b] magic.[p/] They're extremely potent at unleashing energy,
Aster: In the wrong hands,[p/] they could produce an explosion with the power to level an entire block of this city.
Tyrion: ...And there have been mass reports of insurrection here, too.
Whether those reports were true or not,[p/] my teeth clenched at the thought of what someone might do with those crystals.
It was even worse to think about what the Grand General might do to stop that from happening.
Player: Runestone
Aster: That's—!
Tyrion: It’s an Attorney’s Runestone.[p/] It's—
Aster: It's proof that you're a certified attorney!
Aster: I've never seen one of these up close before!
Celeste: Really?[p/] I thought the Crown Prince would be the first person to have a Runestone like this.
Aster: I have a Runestone,[p/][s/] but it's not an [i]Attorney's[/i] Runestone!
Tyrion: Finally![p/][s/] Someone that understands the difference!
Celeste: ...
Player: Tyrion
Aster: I can't believe I'm meeting Tyrion Cuthbert!
Tyrion: I appreciate the admiration,[p/] but I don't see what's so special about me.
Aster: Well, I've been an admirer of Ruby Tymora for a while,[p/] but your story is far more inspiring!
Aster: ...You rose above the tragedy that befell your mother,[p/] and became a force against the nobility.
Tyrion: ...How do you know about that?
Aster: I read about your story in the paper—[p/]a grieving child,[p/] who walked into the woods in despair.
Aster: That story made quite an impression on me.
Aster: When I saw the story of Ms. McCoy's trial,[p/] I recognized you immediately.
Aster: I'm so glad that you survived.
Tyrion: ...
Celeste: ...?
Player: Celeste
Aster: I never imagined that I'd meet the Crimson Demon of the Eastern Kingdoms.
Celeste: Okay,[p/] how the hell did you find out about that nickname?
Aster: O-[p/][s/]Oh,[p/] uh—
Aster: My position as the Crown Prince makes me privy to stories like that.
Tyrion: ...What is the "Crimson Demon", anyways?
Celeste: ...I don't want to talk about it.
I decided to drop the subject.
Player: Aria
Aster: Ah,[p/] Lady Steelwind.[p/] I'm glad to see my fiancée is growing into her role.
Player: Ruby
Aster: Is Ruby Tymora here as well?
Aster: I was wondering if you could put in a good word for me.[p/] I've been wanting her autograph.
Tyrion: [i]You're[/i] the Crown Prince.[p/] Couldn't you just ask her yourself?
Aster: I-[p/][s/]I've tried,[p/] but the last time I saw her,[p/] she had very[p/] [i]strong[/i] words for me.
That [i]did[/i] make sense.[p/] She had more animosity towards the aristocracy than [i]I[/i] did.
Player: Lloyd
Aster: I heard about your chess match with the Duke of Commerce.
Aster: I wish I could have seen it myself!
Tyrion: There wasn't much to see.[p/] For all his arrogance,[p/] The Duke was a pushover.
Aster: [i]*sigh*[/i][p/] That just makes me regret it more.[p/] I would've loved to see that weasel put in his place.
Tyrion: ...Isn't he one of the kingdom's dukes?[p/] I'd thought you two would be more amicable.
Aster: No one knows more about the oppression of the Four Pillars than I do.
...What?
Player: Coraline
Aster: [i]*sigh*[/i][p/] Coraline Sibyl:[p/] the Minister of Justice.
Tyrion: ...It doesn't sound like you have a high opinion of her.
Aster: She's the most powerful of the Four Pillar Lords,[p/] but she's also the most apathetic.
Aster: Our system of governance is broken.[p/] She has the power to change it,[p/] yet she and her House of Lords do nothing.
Tyrion: ...If you care so much,[p/] [i]you[/i] have the ability to change it.
Tyrion: You're a part of the royal family, for crying out loud.
Aster: One [i]would[/i] think that, wouldn't they?
Player: Lucio
Aster: ...I would appreciate it if you didn't speak of the Grand General unless necessary.
Tyrion: ...I take it you don't care for him?
Aster: ...How could I?[p/] My heart aches for the innocent people he's slaughtered.
Aster: All because of these supposed "insurrections".
Tyrion: ...If you really feel that strongly about it,[p/] why hasn't the royal family done anything to stop him?
Aster: House Steelwind controls the only military force equipped to maintain order in Wyverngarde.
Aster: I can't say more,[p/] but their mages are also the only thing standing between us and war with the other kingdoms.
Aster: They are our greatest asset,[p/] while also being our greatest failure.
Player: Aster
Tyrion: I've read a lot of stories about the Crown Prince in the paper,[p/] but they never mention you by name.
Aster: ...That's intentional.[p/] I prefer not to have my name associated with my title.
Celeste: You've done a lot of charity work for the less fortunate, haven't you?
Aster: Yes,[p/] we've hosted so [i]many[/i] fundraisers for them.
Tyrion: ...You're not a fan of fundraisers.
Aster: I'm not a fan of aristocrats who think they can alleviate their guilt by giving me their spare gold.
Player: UnionTensions
Aster: Hm...[p/] I'm sad to say that tensions such as these are rampant throughout the kingdom.
Aster: It's just the same conflict between the commoners and the aristocrats that I've always seen.
Tyrion: Dracogen isn't exactly an aristocrat.[p/] He's just a merchant.
Aster: In the eyes of the commonfolk,[p/] there's very little difference.[p/] He has wealth and power,[p/] while they do not.
Tyrion: But I think he [i]is[/i] trying to help them.[p/] I'm fairly certain these "tensions" are just fabricated stories.
Aster: ...Is that so?
Aster: Even so,[p/] I think the damage is already done.
Aster: I can only hope Mr. Dracogen's intentions are as noble as you claim they are.
Player: ShipRep|Blockade
Player: Treason
Aster: ...I can certainly sympathize with the insurrectionists' displeasure with the kingdom.
Aster: With what the Grand General has been doing in the countryside,[p/] this was inevitable.
Tyrion: ...If you sympathize with them so much,[p/] why hasn't anything been done?
Aster: I won't blame you for saying that,[p/] but there's only so much I can do.
Aster: I hate to say it,[p/] but the royal family has a lot less influence than you'd think,[p/] especially when it comes to the Four Pillars.
Aster: My family holds the crown,[p/] but the dukes and duchesses hold everything else.
...Was that really true?
Player: RikaTest2
Aster: A mysterious, magical explosion pushed Mr. Waye off that catwalk...
Tyrion: There aren't any magical traces there either.
Aster: It's quite the mystery, isn't it?
Aster: Although,[p/] I have no doubts that you'll be able to solve it, Mr. Cuthbert.
Hopefully,[p/] I could live up to his expectations.
Player: Default
Tyrion: Aster, what can you tell me about this?
Aster: My apologies...[p/] I don't think I can help you with that.
Tyrion: Okay, I think that answers all of my questions.[p/] Thank you, Aster.
Aster: Of course![p/][s/] I'm just happy to help.
I was hoping that the Crown Prince had spotted the thief,[p/] but all he saw was some hairless rodent.
I would just need to look into my other leads and hope that would lead somewhere.
Either way,[p/] it was time to MOVE somewhere else.
Player: Card
Tyrion: It's some kind of tarot card.
Mosourika: A customer must have accidentally left it here.
Tyrion: "The Magician..."
Mosourika: The Magician is associated with the power of transformation and manifestation.
Mosourika: And it's in its upright position,[p/] which is associated with the resourcefulness to harness one's inner potential.
Mosourika: It often symbolizes a person who possesses great skill,[p/] creativity,[p/] and mastery over their craft.
Tyrion: …I never knew you knew about these things.
Mosourika: Ha![p/][s/] Of course, I do!
Mosourika: The power of the magician is the power to shape reality through the power of will!
Mosourika: Doesn't [i]that[/i] remind you of someone?
Tyrion: No...[p/] Not really.
Mosourika: Hey!
Player: Barrels
Tyrion: There are some barrels stacked in the corner here.
Celeste: I wonder what's inside.
Celeste: Wait...[p/] Rika.[p/] You wouldn't happen to have Lotus Juice, do you?
Mosourika: "Lotus Juice"? [p/] What's that?
Celeste: [i]*sigh*[/i][p/] Never mind.
Tyrion: What happened to the Lotus Juice that you "received" from Miriam?
Celeste: I drank all of it…
Already...?[p/] She'd only had it for a month.
Player: Table
Tyrion: This table looks [i]really[/i] well-made.
Mosourika: You like it?[p/] I commissioned a local carpenter to have my tables custom-made.
Celeste: It was probably worth it.
Celeste: They fit with the rustic vibe you've got going on here.
Player: Plant
Rika seemed to have decorated the entire café with several plants and flowers.
The green added a fresh and earthy look to the restaurant.
Player: Lantern
There were artificery lights hanging from the ceiling.
Tyrion: Your business must be doing pretty well if you can afford artificery like this.
Mosourika: That's just one of the benefits I get from working with the Dracogen Trading Company.
Mosourika: Most of the merchants connect to the artificery ley line.
Player: Shelf
There was a shelf full of drinks,[p/] mugs,[p/] and sugar behind the restaurant counter.
It must've been where Rika's employees prepared coffee to be served.
Player: The Court Case
Tyrion: Alright,[p/] let's get to work.
Tyrion: You said that you saw someone cast a spell that pushed Justin into the vats.
Tyrion: Can you tell me exactly what you saw?[p/] I need every detail you can remember.
Mosourika: It was kind of hard to see.[p/] I was on the ground level,[p/] and he was up on the catwalk.
Mosourika: I think I saw him yelling at someone from up there.
Mosourika: ...That's when I saw a flash of light.
Mosourika: There was a short burst of magical energy that ripped the guardrail apart.
Mosourika: Justin happened to be standing right next to the top of a nearby vat.[p/] He fell right into it.
Mosourika: I told one of the workers what I saw,[p/] and they went to try to pull him out.
Mosourika: But I guess it was too late.[p/] Those chemicals must have been really toxic.
They weren't just toxic.[p/] They were strong enough to completely melt down his body.
Mosourika: ...There's actually something else.
Mosourika: I saw Justin drop something from his hands before he fell into that vat.
Mosourika: After they pulled him out,[p/] I tried looking for it and cast [b]Detect Magic[/b].
Mosourika: That's when I found [i]this[/i] on the ground below the walkway.
Tyrion: A leather strap?
I looked at the strap more closely.
It had a buckle on it,[p/] but nothing to connect it with.
:The numerals: "VI",[p/] were engraved on it.[p/] I was really confused as to what the purpose of this was.
Mosourika: I saw a blue glow when I looked at it.
Celeste: It was cyan?[p/] That's the color of [b]conjuration[b/] traces.
Mosourika: I tried to give it to the investigators,[p/] but they didn't think it was important.
I guess that made sense.[p/] They were considering this an accident,[p/] not a murder.
Player: Why were you there?
Tyrion: Why [i]were[/i] you at the refinery anyway?
Tyrion: You don't work there, do you?
Mosourika: ...I was delivering Justin's breakfast.
Mosourika: He always skips it.[p/] It really annoys me,[p/] so I started bringing some of my pastries over every morning.
Celeste: What?[p/] Breakfast is the most important meal of the day!
Tyrion: ...So important that you ate it multiple times yesterday.
Celeste: I have a high metabolism, okay?
Tyrion: ...Anyways,[p/] isn't it kind of dangerous to let a civilian walk around a factory like that?
Mosourika: Ha![p/][s/] I am no ordinary civilian!
Mosourika: No one there would dare to bar my visit to the refinery.
Tyrion: ...Which means that you don't usually visit the Refinery.
Mosourika: ...Yeah,[p/] I usually just meet Justin in the freight area.
Mosourika: But he didn't show up that day,[p/] so I got worried and went looking around.
Mosourika: The people who work there recognized me and let me in.
Mosourika: ...That's when I saw him fall into the vat.
Player: The Spell
Tyrion: Did you recognize the spell Justin was hit with?
Mosourika: I [i]am[/i] a mage,[p/] but I've never seen a spell like that before.
Mosourika: It happened instantly,[p/] and it didn't look like an explosion or anything.
Mosourika: It kind of looked more like...[p/] an expanding prism.
Celeste: ...A prism?
Tyrion: Does that match with any spells that [i]you're[/i] familiar with?
Celeste: I specialize in [b]transmutation[/b] spells,[p/] but any of the spells I know that could create a cube wouldn't expand instantly like that.
Tyrion: The problem is that the Inquisition didn't find [i]any[/i] traces of magic on the walkway...
Tyrion: It couldn't have been an [b]evocation[/b] spell or a [b]transmutation[/b] spell.
Celeste: I actually wanted to ask you about that.[p/] Why would an explosive [b]evocation[/b] spell leave traces of magic?
Celeste: Magical traces are only left on the objects they are targeting.[p/] Something like a [b]fireball[/b] spell isn't technically targeting anything.
Tyrion: That's correct,[p/] but every [b]evocation[/b] spell produces some kind of residue.
Tyrion: In the case of [b]fireball[/b],[p/] its magical traces would be left on whatever it had burned or melted.
Tyrion: If someone used an [b]evocation[/b] spell powerful enough to rip the guardrail apart,[p/] they would've found traces of [b]evocation[/b] there.
Mosourika: Wow,[p/] I never knew how technical these things were.
Tyrion: We're lucky they are.[p/] Otherwise,[p/] I'd have a lot less evidence to work with.
Mosourika: ...If what you're saying is true,[p/] was I wrong about what I saw?
Tyrion: I wouldn't say that.
Tyrion: I've been doing this long enough to know that there's always an explanation for oddities like this.
Mosourika: A gem with strange carvings?[p/] Is it cursed?
Tyrion: It’s an Attorney’s Runestone.[p/] It proves that I’m a certified attorney.
Tyrion: It’s fitted with artificery.[p/][s/] I can use it to take pictures of evidence and crime scenes.
Mosourika: ...Really?
Mosourika: Do you know where I can get one of those?
Tyrion: You have to be an attorney to have one.
Celeste: Tyrion...[p/] People use Runestones for things other than practicing law.
Mosourika: I'm sorry I had to drag you into this.[p/] I didn't know who else to turn to.
Tyrion: Don't worry about it.[p/] You've saved me more times than I can count.
Mosourika: Wait...[p/] When did I save you?
Tyrion: ...Did you forget?[p/] It's how we met.
Celeste: What happened?
Tyrion: I was always getting harassed by some of the older kids by the harbor.
Tyrion: One day,[p/] they tried to steal the allowance my mother gave me.
Tyrion: You stepped in and stopped them.
Mosourika: Ah,[p/] I remember those cretins now.
Mosourika: They had no choice but to flee from the weight of my majesty.
Tyrion: She basically knew how to scream in the most alarming way.
Tyrion: It sounded like something between a rabid fox and a screeching baby.
Mosourika: A-[p/][s/]Ah, yes.[p/] My battle cry alone was enough to end battles before they started.
Mosourika: ...It drew the attention of law enforcement.[p/] They thought a monster had broken into the city.
Tyrion: I think that's when I became your "apprentice".[p/] We'd always go on adventures together through the city.
Mosourika: Yes,[p/] and look how much you've grown![p/] I'm glad I put you on the path to greatness!
Mosourika: ...
Mosourika: ...I really miss those days.
Tyrion: ...Me too.
Mosourika: ...After all this is over,[p/] let's go on another adventure.
Mosourika: You should come with us, Celeste.
Celeste: Yeah![p/][s/] I'm always up for an adventure!
Mosourika: Celeste,[p/] I love your sword!
Mosourika: Is that [i]bone[/i]?
Celeste: Yeah![p/][s/] How did you know?
Mosourika: I could tell by the texture.[p/] I've held some really exotic blades being sold by some of the travelling merchants here.
Celeste: Yeah,[p/] it was carved from the bones of a Purple Worm.[p/] It's unbreakable.
Mosourika: ...I want one.
Celeste: I'm sure you can find your own sword from those travelling merchants.
Mosourika: ...I said I [i]held[/i] those blades.[p/] Affording them is another matter.
Mosourika: ...How [i]was[/i] living with Ruby Tymora?
Mosourika: From the way the papers wrote about her,[p/] she doesn't sound like the kind of person that could raise a ten-year-old.
Tyrion: ...Those are just baseless rumors being spread by the nobility.
Tyrion: They're just trying to discredit her in the public eye.
Mosourika: Oh, okay.[p/] That makes sense.
Mosourika: They made her sound like some ruthless maniac who'll do whatever it takes to win.
...I wanted to deny that,[p/] so why didn't I?
